_meta: Independently compiled by the getbased project from primary published literature. Each entry cites a PubMed ID (PMID) for the source study. NOT derived from MitoTox or any third-party database.

effects.t: supratherapeutic dose; primary low-dose target debated
candidate primary target at therapeutic dose (Madiraju 2014)
high dose only — 81 mg cardioprotective dose has no measurable effect
from ROS damage (indirect via antioxidant effect, not direct activity enhancement)
low dose — functional effect replicated, but direct SIRT1 binding contested (Pacholec 2010); likely upstream via AMPK
withaferin A, selective for cancer cells
via PGC-1alpha; validated in sarcopenic elderly RCTs
low dose <2 mg/kg — alternative electron carrier, bypasses Complex I/III
high dose >10 mg/kg — pro-oxidant direction at elevated doses
via autophagy induction and hypusination of eIF5A
most potent natural senolytic per Mayo screen (Yousefzadeh 2018)
